title: Onboarding Friction Loop
summary: Finds and removes the highest-friction step in a new-user onboarding journey.
steps: Define the activation event and map every step a new user must complete to reach it.
Use analytics, session evidence, support tickets, and fresh-user walkthroughs to identify the biggest drop-off or confusion point.
Fix one friction point with the smallest testable product, copy, docs, or defaults change.
Measure the same activation and qualitative signals after the change.
Repeat only if the next friction point is clearly higher impact than polishing the last one.
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Onboarding Friction Loop for [journey, activation_event, evidence_sources].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Define the activation event and map every step a new user must complete to reach it.
- Use analytics, session evidence, support tickets, and fresh-user walkthroughs to identify the biggest drop-off or confusion point.
- Fix one friction point with the smallest testable product, copy, docs, or defaults change.
- Measure the same activation and qualitative signals after the change.
- Repeat only if the next friction point is clearly higher impact than polishing the last one.

Quality gates before completion:
- activation event defined
- friction point supported by evidence
- one change tested
- same metric measured after

Stop when one of these is true:
- activation target reached
- data is insufficient
- next fix needs product approval

Finish with:
- journey map
- friction evidence
- change summary
- before-after metric

Escalate for human approval before: changing pricing gates, collecting new user data, altering legal consent flows.
